title: How Dear To Me The Hour.
content:
How dear to me the hour when daylight dies,
        And sunbeams melt along the silent sea,
    For then sweet dreams of other days arise,
        And memory breathes her vesper sigh to thee.

    And, as I watch the line of light, that plays
        Along the smooth wave toward the burning west,
    I long to tread that golden path of rays,
        And think 'twould lead to some bright isle of rest.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Thomas Moore delivers a powerful performance in "How Dear To Me The Hour."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
